Title: Copper ions suppress abscisic acid biosynthesis to enhance defence against Phytophthora infestans in potato

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes defence against P. infestans in this study
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Several ACS genes are rapidly upregulated within minutes after CuSO4 treatment
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR for transcript induction; ET measurement by gas chromatography; ACC treatment phenocopy
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cu2+ rapidly induces ACS gene expression associated with transient ethylene production and enhanced disease resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cu2+ rapidly upregulates several potato ACS genes. 2. ET production peaks early after CuSO4 treatment. 3. ACC treatment enhances resistance similarly to Cu2+, implicating ACS-mediated ET biosynthesis in defence.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream leverage points to induce ET-dependent immunity, though specific causal ACS member(s) remain unresolved
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ethylene activation may affect growth and stress-response balance; specific tradeoffs not resolved here
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper did not functionally validate individual ACS genes by silencing/overexpression; therefore grouped as common rather than pathway core genes

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates stomatal closure, drought tolerance, and modulates plant defence responses
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early/entry step of ABA biosynthesis from carotenoid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Reported as an important entry-point enzyme for ABA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels decrease at 2 h and 24 h after CuSO4 treatment; higher in StEIN2- and StEIN3-silenced leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient overexpression vector pCXSN; native promoter analyzed; CaMV 35S not used for StABA1 expression in reported promoter-binding assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10 μM CuSO4 treatment; ABA treatment; fluridone treatment; Phytophthora infestans infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; HPLC-MS/MS ABA quantification; VIGS knockdown; transient overexpression; disease index assays; ChIP-qPCR and EMSA for upstream regulator binding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown enhances late blight resistance by reducing ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cu2+ suppresses StABA1 transcription in potato leaves. 2. StABA1 knockdown reduces ABA-associated susceptibility and enhances resistance to late blight. 3. StEIN3 directly binds the StABA1 promoter, placing StABA1 downstream of ethylene signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Silencing or editing StABA1 may enhance potato late blight resistance, but likely with drought-sensitivity tradeoffs due to reduced ABA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promotes stomatal opening, water loss, and drought sensitivity when ABA biosynthesis is reduced
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of the core ABA biosynthetic genes repressed by Cu2+; directly repressed by StEIN3 in vivo and in vitro

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates stomatal closure, drought tolerance, and modulates plant defence responses
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cleavage step in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Reported as the limiting step of ABA synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript levels decrease after CuSO4 treatment; increase in StEIN2- and StEIN3-silenced leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10 μM CuSO4 treatment; ABA treatment; fluridone treatment; P. infestans infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; HPLC-MS/MS ABA quantification; VIGS knockdown; transient overexpression; disease index assays; ChIP-qPCR and EMSA for upstream regulator binding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced StNCED1 expression decreases ABA accumulation and enhances resistance to P. infestans; overexpression attenuates Cu2+-mediated resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cu2+ suppresses StNCED1 transcription in potato leaves. 2. StNCED1 knockdown enhances resistance to late blight, showing ABA promotes susceptibility. 3. StEIN3 directly binds the StNCED1 promoter, linking ethylene signaling to ABA suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulating StNCED1 is a potential strategy to improve late blight resistance, with caution for drought-related penalties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced ABA biosynthesis is associated with greater stomatal opening, water loss, and drought sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains multiple predicted EIN3-binding sites; direct binding validated by ChIP and EMSA

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates stomatal closure, drought tolerance, and defence tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis repression downstream of ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulation of ABA biosynthetic gene expression through ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10 μM CuSO4 treatment; P. infestans infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; RT-qPCR; ABA immunofluorescence quantification; ET measurement; disease index assays; genetic epistasis with StABA1/StNCED1 silencing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: StEIN2 knockdown increases StABA1/StNCED1 expression and ABA accumulation, causing greater susceptibility to late blight
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required upstream ethylene signaling component that suppresses ABA biosynthesis and is necessary for Cu2+-mediated resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StEIN2 positively contributes to potato resistance to P. infestans. 2. Silencing StEIN2 elevates StABA1 and StNCED1 transcript abundance and ABA accumulation. 3. Double silencing with StABA1 or StNCED1 partially restores resistance, placing StEIN2 upstream of ABA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing StEIN2 signaling may improve disease resistance by constraining ABA biosynthesis, but stress-balance effects should be evaluated
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ethylene–ABA antagonism may alter drought responses and stomatal behavior
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cu2+-mediated disease resistance fails in StEIN2-silenced plants; ET level in EIN2-VIGS plants is reduced relative to control

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates stomatal closure, drought tolerance, and defence tradeoffs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: EIN3/EIL family transcription factor; binds EIN3-binding sites (AYGWAYCT)
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis repression downstream of ethylene signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression of ABA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses StABA1 and StNCED1 transcription by promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 10 μM CuSO4 treatment; P. infestans infection
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; RT-qPCR; ABA immunofluorescence quantification; disease assays; ChIP-qPCR with FLAG-StEIN3; EMSA with MBP-StEIN3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: StEIN3 suppresses ABA biosynthetic gene expression, lowers ABA accumulation, and promotes resistance to P. infestans
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct transcriptional link connecting ethylene signaling to suppression of ABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. StEIN3 is required for Cu2+-enhanced late blight resistance. 2. Silencing StEIN3 increases StABA1/StNCED1 expression and ABA accumulation. 3. StEIN3 directly binds the promoters of StABA1 and StNCED1 in vivo and in vitro.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting StEIN3 or its binding modules may enable tuning of ethylene–ABA crosstalk for disease resistance engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced repression of ABA biosynthesis may increase water loss and drought sensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter binding verified at specific fragments by ChIP and EMSA; serves as direct regulator rather than merely signaling component
